optimizer_switches.name: materialization
optimizer_switches.explanation: A subquery or derived table is executed once and its result stored in a temporary table for reuse. Watch tmp_table_size / max_heap_table_size — it may spill to disk.

warnings.text: Full table scan: departments (8 rows), employees (2000 rows)
2 temp table(s) (Materialize) — up to 2000 rows; may spill to disk
2 sort operation(s) — 2000 rows; may use disk-based filesort

suggestions.action: Add indexes on filter/join columns to avoid full table scans
Increase tmp_table_size / max_heap_table_size to keep temp tables in memory
Increase sort_buffer_size or add an ordered index to avoid filesort

executive_summary: Query scans 2 tables, materializes 2 temp tables and sorts the result; examines ~4,008 rows to return 24 in 3.8 ms. Main finding: full scan of employees (2,000 rows).

query.raw: with `dept_avg` as (/* select#2 */ select `demodb`.`employees`.`dept_id` AS `dept_id`,avg(`demodb`.`employees`.`salary`) AS `avg_sal`,count(0) AS `n` from `demodb`.`employees` group by `demodb`.`employees`.`dept_id`), `top_paid` as (/* select#3 */ select `e`.`id` AS `id`,`e`.`name` AS `name`,`e`.`dept_id` AS `dept_id`,`e`.`salary` AS `salary`,row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY `demodb`.`e`.`dept_id` ORDER BY `demodb`.`e`.`salary` desc )  AS `rn` from `demodb`.`employees` `e`) /* select#1 */ select `d`.`name` AS `dept`,`da`.`avg_sal` AS `avg_sal`,`tp`.`name` AS `top_earner`,`tp`.`salary` AS `salary` from `demodb`.`departments` `d` join `dept_avg` `da` join `top_paid` `tp` where ((`tp`.`dept_id` = `d`.`id`) and (`da`.`dept_id` = `d`.`id`) and (`tp`.`rn` <= 3)) order by `da`.`avg_sal` desc
query.beautified:
with dept_avg as (/*
select#2 */
select demodb.employees.dept_id AS dept_id,avg(demodb.employees.salary) AS avg_sal,count(0) AS n
from demodb.employees
group by demodb.employees.dept_id), top_paid as (/*
select#3 */
select e.id AS id,e.name AS name,e.dept_id AS dept_id,e.salary AS salary,row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY demodb.e.dept_id
ORDER BY demodb.e.salary desc ) AS rn
from demodb.employees e) /*
select#1 */
select d.name AS dept,da.avg_sal AS avg_sal,tp.name AS top_earner,tp.salary AS salary
from demodb.departments d
  join dept_avg da
  join top_paid tp
where ((tp.dept_id = d.id) and (da.dept_id = d.id) and (tp.rn <= 3))
order by da.avg_sal desc

teach_hooks.note: Index scan on employees using idx_dept (employees)
teach_hooks.query_sql: with `dept_avg` as (/* select#2 */ select `demodb`.`employees`.`dept_id` AS `dept_id`,avg(`demodb`.`employees`.`salary`) AS `avg_sal`,count(0) AS `n` from `demodb`.`employees` group by `demodb`.`employees`.`dept_id`), `top_paid` as (/* select#3 */ select `e`.`id` AS `id`,`e`.`name` AS `name`,`e`.`dept_id` AS `dept_id`,`e`.`salary` AS `salary`,row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY `demodb`.`e`.`dept_id` ORDER BY `demodb`.`e`.`salary` desc )  AS `rn` from `demodb`.`employees` `e`) /* select#1 */ select `d`.`name` AS `dept`,`da`.`avg_sal` AS `avg_sal`,`tp`.`name` AS `top_earner`,`tp`.`salary` AS `salary` from `demodb`.`departments` `d` join `dept_avg` `da` join `top_paid` `tp` where ((`tp`.`dept_id` = `d`.`id`) and (`da`.`dept_id` = `d`.`id`) and (`tp`.`rn` <= 3)) order by `da`.`avg_sal` desc

complexity.rationale: Filesort: MySQL sorts the output in the server layer. Priority-queue filesort (LIMIT) is not detected today — we report the upper bound.
Indexed nested loop: each outer row probes the inner table via an index descent.
Full table scan: the storage engine returns every row; cost scales with the table size.
Index lookup (ref): one B+tree descent then a sequential walk over k matching index entries. k = matching rows for the indexed predicate.

complexity.big_o: build: O(m)  •  scan: O(1) per probe, O(rows) to fetch all
complexity.rationale: Materialize is a two-phase operator. The build pays the inner subquery's cost ONCE; each scan of the temp table is effectively constant time. Don't compound build cost by loops.

build_complexity.rationale: Materialize build: cost = cost of the source subquery over m rows.

scan_complexity.big_o: O(1) per probe, O(rows) to fetch all
scan_complexity.rationale: Materialize scan: reads the pre-computed temp table. Each probe is constant time once the table is in memory.

complexity.rationale: Filesort: MySQL sorts the output in the server layer. Priority-queue filesort (LIMIT) is not detected today — we report the upper bound.
Full table scan: the storage engine returns every row; cost scales with the table size.
Index lookup (ref): one B+tree descent then a sequential walk over k matching index entries. k = matching rows for the indexed predicate.

complexity.big_o: build: O(n log n)  •  scan: O(1) per probe, O(rows) to fetch all
complexity.rationale: Materialize is a two-phase operator. The build pays the inner subquery's cost ONCE; each scan of the temp table is effectively constant time. Don't compound build cost by loops.

build_complexity.rationale: Sort-based aggregation: MySQL sorts by the grouping key before collapsing duplicates.

scan_complexity.big_o: O(1) per probe, O(rows) to fetch all
scan_complexity.rationale: Materialize scan: reads the pre-computed temp table. Each probe is constant time once the table is in memory.

complexity.rationale: Sort-based aggregation: MySQL sorts by the grouping key before collapsing duplicates.
Index scan: reads every entry of the index in key order. Cheaper than a table scan because the index is smaller, but still O(n).
